comment: Anakinra is the reference benchmark for IL-1Ra therapeutic plasma exposure. The dose-modeling GREEN/RED gate is whether inhaled mRNA-IL-1Ra can plausibly reach an equivalent plasma AUC. Anakinra PK from Kineret prescribing information and published PK studies.

anakinra_pk.comment: Cmax range 1.3-1.8 ug/mL across studies; central estimate 1.5
anakinra_pk.auc_calculation: Approximated from Cmax * (half_life / ln2) * 1.44 absorption factor. Conservative central estimate.
anakinra_pk.single_flare_dose_anakinra_off_label: 100 mg daily x 3 days typical off-label gout flare regimen; sometimes 100 mg BID for first 24h. Total flare dose 300-600 mg over 3-5 days.
anakinra_pk.source: Kineret USPI; Yang et al. 2003; Granowitz 1992; Janssen 2019 Lancet Rheumatol gout meta-analysis

target_for_inhaled_mrna.comment: What inhaled-mRNA-IL-1Ra must achieve to clear the GREEN gate
target_for_inhaled_mrna.comment_on_minimum: Anakinra trough (Cmin) is 0.05 ug/mL and clinical effect is sustained. Sub-Cmax exposure is therapeutically meaningful. A Cmax of 0.5 ug/mL (1/3 anakinra Cmax) plausibly suffices for IL-1 blockade in acute gout context.
target_for_inhaled_mrna.comment_on_target: Anakinra-equivalent — the upper bar. The GREEN verdict requires the inhaled-mRNA approach to plausibly reach this target across the prior space.

canakinumab_economic_benchmark.comment: The often-cited '$300K/year canakinumab' benchmark assumes ~10-12 flares/yr or autoinflammatory chronic dosing. Real-world gout-flare-only cost lands $50K-300K/yr depending on flare frequency. Either way, the order of magnitude is the benchmark.
canakinumab_economic_benchmark.source: Canakinumab Ilaris prescribing info; AHFS pricing tables; FDA approval 2023
